shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that those who eat the happy leftovers of giving reach the forever truth while those who never give have no real home here or after. This verse shows why sharing is important for the big journey. For children this is like finishing a shared snack and feeling full of joy inside. For example a child who offers part of his treat to friends feels connected and happy forever. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the remains of kind giving nourish the heart and lead to the eternal home. Without giving one feels empty inside. This beautiful lesson of transcendental knowledge turns every share into a magic step. The verse makes you curious to give more. Practice: offer a little of your snack or toy each day with a smile and feel the big happy grow inside you.

shlokas.sanskrit:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे |
कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ||
shlokas.transliteration:
evaṁ bahu-vidhā yajñā vitatā brahmaṇo mukhe
karma-jān viddhi tān sarvān evaṁ jñātvā vimokṣyase
shlokas.translation: All these different kinds of sacrifice are born of work and are described in the Vedas. Knowing them as such you will become liberated.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna states that all varieties of sacrifice arise from action and are explained in the scriptures. Understanding this truth leads to liberation. This verse summarizes the many paths of yajna. For people in their thirties exploring different spiritual practices this verse offers freedom through knowledge. For example someone who studies various forms of service and sees them all as expressions of the same divine principle feels liberated from rigid rules. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that every sacrifice is rooted in karma yet leads to the same goal when performed with awareness. This beautiful overview of transcendental knowledge removes confusion. The verse makes readers eager to apply the insight. Practice: recognize all your duties as forms of sacrifice and perform them with understanding so you attain true freedom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna states that all kinds of offerings come from action and are laid out in the ancient texts. Knowing this truth sets you free. This verse wraps the many ways of giving. For young adults mixing practices this verse brings easy liberation. For example someone who sees journaling meditation and helping others as different offerings feels light and unbound. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows every sacrifice grows from action yet points to the same light when done with awareness. This beautiful map of transcendental knowledge clears the way. The verse makes you eager to live it. Practice: view all your moves as offerings and do them with clear knowledge so freedom flows naturally.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna states that all kinds of gifts come from play and are told in the wise books. Knowing this truth makes you free. This verse wraps the many happy ways of sharing. For children this is like seeing drawing singing and helping as different gifts all leading to the big smile. For example a child who plays in many ways yet knows they all make the heart happy feels free. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches every giving grows from action yet opens the same joy when done with heart. This beautiful picture of transcendental knowledge removes worry. The verse makes you curious to play freely. Practice: see all your games as gifts and enjoy them with understanding so your heart stays light and free.

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप |
सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śreyān dravya-mayād yajñāj jñāna-yajñaḥ parantapa
sarvaṁ karmākhilaṁ pārtha jñāne parisamāpyate
shlokas.translation: O subduer of enemies sacrifice performed in knowledge is better than sacrifice performed with material possessions. After all O son of Pṛthā all sacrifices of work culminate in knowledge.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna declares that the sacrifice of knowledge is superior to material offerings. All actions ultimately end in knowledge. This verse elevates wisdom above ritual. For people in their thirties investing in learning this verse validates lifelong growth. For example a professional who studies ethical principles and applies them finds every task transformed into wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that jnana yajna purifies the soul completely and all karma converges there. This beautiful culmination of transcendental knowledge shows the goal. The verse makes readers eager to pursue understanding. Practice: offer your learning and insights as the highest sacrifice so every action leads to wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna declares that the offering of knowledge beats material gifts. All actions finally rest in knowledge. This verse lifts wisdom above stuff. For young adults growing through content and experience this verse feels right. For example someone who shares insights online and lives them sees every post and choice become pure understanding. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows jnana yajna cleans everything and all moves meet there. This beautiful end of transcendental knowledge points the way. The verse makes you eager to dive deeper. Practice: give your learning and thoughts as the best offering so every step turns into real wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna declares that the gift of knowledge is better than toy offerings. All play finally rests in knowledge. This verse lifts smart knowing above things. For children this is like sharing stories and lessons being the best gift. For example a child who tells kind tales and lives them feels every game turn wise. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches the knowledge gift cleans the heart and all play meets there. This beautiful finish of transcendental knowledge guides the fun. The verse makes you curious to learn more. Practice: offer your stories and lessons as the happiest gift so every play becomes bright wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया |
उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
tad viddhi praṇipātena paripraśnena sevayā
upadekṣyanti te jñānaṁ jñāninas tattva-darśinaḥ
shlokas.translation: Just try to learn the truth by approaching a spiritual master inquire from him submissively and render service unto him. The self realized souls can impart knowledge unto you because they have seen the truth.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna advises approaching a realized teacher with humility inquiry and service to receive transcendental knowledge. This verse outlines the method of learning. For people in their thirties seeking mentors this verse provides the perfect approach. For example a young leader who humbly serves and questions an experienced guide gains profound insights that transform his career. Detailed meaning: Krishna emphasizes that true knowledge comes only from those who have realized the truth through surrender and service. This beautiful instruction of transcendental knowledge ensures authentic transmission. The verse makes readers eager to find and approach such guides. Practice: seek a genuine teacher with respect and service so the highest wisdom can be received directly.
shlokas.genz: Krishna advises going to a realized teacher with humility real questions and genuine service to gain transcendental knowledge. This verse gives the clear way to learn. For young adults building networks this verse feels authentic. For example someone who respectfully helps and asks a wise mentor receives deep truths that shift their entire perspective. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that real knowledge flows only from those who have seen the truth through humble connection. This beautiful method of transcendental knowledge protects the purity. The verse makes you eager to connect rightly. Practice: approach a true guide with open heart and service so the light of wisdom reaches you.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna advises going to a wise big friend with a bow kind questions and helpful service to receive transcendental knowledge. This verse gives the happy way to learn. For children this is like sitting near a caring teacher asking nicely and helping so the best stories come. For example a child who listens with love and helps the teacher feels the magic inside. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows that true knowing comes only from those who have seen the light through gentle service. This beautiful way of transcendental knowledge keeps the joy pure. The verse makes you curious to meet such friends. Practice: go to a kind teacher with respect and help so the happiest wisdom fills your heart.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव |
येन भूतान्यशेषाणि द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yaj jñātvā na punar moham evaṁ yāsyasi pāṇḍava
yena bhūtāny aśeṣāṇi drakṣyasy ātmani atho mayi
shlokas.translation: Having obtained this knowledge you will never again be deluded O son of Pāṇḍu. By this knowledge you will see all beings in the self and then in Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna assures that after receiving this knowledge one will never fall into illusion again and will see all beings in the self and ultimately in the divine. This verse promises the vision of oneness. For people in their thirties seeking clarity in relationships this verse brings unity. For example a leader who sees every team member as part of the same divine reality leads with compassion and wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that transcendental knowledge destroys delusion and reveals the unity of all existence in the self and in God. This beautiful realization ends separation. The verse makes readers eager to attain this vision. Practice: contemplate the oneness taught here until you perceive every being as divine and feel the peace of unity.
shlokas.genz: Krishna assures that once you receive this knowledge you will never get lost in confusion again and will see every being in the self and then in the divine. This verse promises the view of oneness. For young adults building communities this verse feels connecting. For example someone who views every friend or follower as part of the same big energy creates real belonging. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that transcendental knowledge wipes away illusion and shows all life as one in the self and in God. This beautiful sight ends all apartness. The verse makes you eager to live this view. Practice: meditate on the unity until you feel every soul as divine and experience the flow of connection.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna assures that after getting this knowledge you will never feel confused again and will see every friend in the self and then in the big friend. This verse promises the happy view of oneness. For children this is like knowing all playmates are part of the same big love. For example a child who sees every friend as special feels the whole world smiling. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows that transcendental knowledge removes all mix up and reveals every being as one in the self and in God. This beautiful seeing ends lonely feelings. The verse makes you curious to feel this. Practice: think of the oneness until you see every friend as part of the big love and feel the warm togetherness.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः |
ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुतेर्जुन ||
shlokas.transliteration:
api ced asi pāpebhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāpa-kṛt-tamaḥ
jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasmasāt kurute ’rjuna
shlokas.translation: Even if you are considered the most sinful of all sinners still the fire of knowledge will burn all your reactions to sin O Arjuna.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna assures that even the greatest sinner can be purified by the fire of knowledge which burns all karmic reactions. This verse offers hope to everyone. For people in their thirties carrying past regrets this verse brings complete forgiveness. For example someone who has made serious mistakes in life finds through deep study and application of wisdom that all burdens are lifted. Detailed meaning: Krishna emphasizes the immense purifying power of transcendental knowledge over any past action. This beautiful assurance shows no one is beyond redemption. The verse makes readers eager to ignite this fire within. Practice: immerse yourself in transcendental knowledge daily so that any past reactions are completely burned away.
shlokas.genz: Krishna assures that even if you feel like the biggest mess up the fire of knowledge will burn every heavy karma away. This verse gives real hope to everyone. For young adults dealing with past choices this verse feels freeing. For example someone who has slipped badly discovers through honest reflection and learning that the weight disappears. Detailed meaning: Krishna highlights how transcendental knowledge acts as a total cleanser no matter the history. This beautiful promise removes all limits. The verse makes you eager to light the fire. Practice: dive into the knowledge every day so old heavy feelings turn to ash.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna assures that even if you feel like the naughtiest the fire of knowledge will burn all sad mistakes away. This verse gives happy hope to everyone. For children this is like knowing that learning the right stories can make every oops disappear. For example a child who feels sorry learns wise words and feels clean again. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that transcendental knowledge is the strongest cleanser for any trouble. This beautiful promise says no one is left out. The verse makes you curious to learn. Practice: listen to good stories every day so any sad feelings turn to happy ash.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन |
ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yathaidhāṁsi samiddho ’gnir bhasmasāt kurute ’rjuna
jñānāgniḥ sarva-karmāṇi bhasmasāt kurute tathā
shlokas.translation: As a blazing fire turns firewood to ashes O Arjuna so does the fire of knowledge burn all reactions to material activities.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna compares the fire of knowledge to a blazing fire that reduces wood to ashes. It burns all karmic reactions completely. This verse uses a vivid analogy for purification. For people in their thirties releasing past burdens this verse provides powerful imagery. For example reflecting on mistakes through wisdom feels like watching old regrets turn to harmless ash. Detailed meaning: Krishna illustrates the total transformative power of transcendental knowledge. Nothing remains after its fire. This beautiful analogy inspires complete surrender to wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to kindle this inner fire. Practice: meditate on the knowledge until it blazes within and consumes every reaction leaving only peace.
shlokas.genz: Krishna compares the fire of knowledge to a big blaze that turns wood to ash. It burns every karma reaction to nothing. This verse gives a strong picture of cleaning. For young adults dropping old stories this verse feels empowering. For example looking back at tough times with new understanding makes them disappear like smoke. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows how transcendental knowledge completely transforms and erases all past energy. This beautiful image motivates full ignition. The verse makes you eager to burn bright. Practice: let the knowledge grow hot inside until it turns every old weight to light ash.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna compares the fire of knowledge to a bright campfire that turns sticks to ash. It burns every play mistake to nothing. This verse gives a fun picture of cleaning. For children this is like watching old sad sticks disappear in happy flames. For example remembering a mistake but learning makes it go poof. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that transcendental knowledge turns all troubles into soft ash. This beautiful picture makes cleaning exciting. The verse makes you curious to watch the fire. Practice: think of the knowledge as a warm fire until it makes every sad feeling turn to happy ash.

shlokas.sanskrit:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते |
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
na hi jñānena sadṛśaṁ pavitram iha vidyate
tat svayaṁ yoga-saṁsiddhaḥ kālenātmani vindati
shlokas.translation: In this world there is nothing so sublime and pure as transcendental knowledge. Such knowledge is the mature fruit of all mysticism and one who has achieved it enjoys it within himself in due course of time.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna declares that nothing in this world is as pure and sublime as transcendental knowledge. It is the perfected fruit of all yoga and is realized within the self in time. This verse glorifies knowledge. For people in their thirties investing in self development this verse affirms the highest investment. For example years of consistent study and practice yield an inner treasure that no external success can match. Detailed meaning: Krishna positions transcendental knowledge as the supreme purifier and the natural outcome of all spiritual paths. This beautiful praise encourages lifelong pursuit. The verse makes readers eager to ripen this fruit. Practice: dedicate time to knowledge and yoga so that the supreme purity matures within you.
shlokas.genz: Krishna declares that nothing here is as pure and high as transcendental knowledge. It is the full fruit of all yoga and blooms inside in its own time. This verse praises knowledge as the best. For young adults growing through experiences this verse feels like the ultimate win. For example steady inner work turns into a quiet treasure that shines brighter than any trend. Detailed meaning: Krishna places transcendental knowledge above all as the natural ripe result of every path. This beautiful praise motivates the journey. The verse makes you eager to let it bloom. Practice: keep feeding your inner growth so the highest purity ripens inside you.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna declares that nothing here is as clean and bright as transcendental knowledge. It is the full happy fruit of all play and grows inside at the right time. This verse praises knowledge as the best. For children this is like knowing the wisest story that makes the heart the cleanest. For example learning good tales turns into a forever happy light inside. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows transcendental knowledge as the sweetest ripe gift of every path. This beautiful praise makes learning exciting. The verse makes you curious to grow it. Practice: keep listening to wise stories so the brightest clean joy grows inside you.

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः |
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śraddhāvāṁl labhate jñānaṁ tat-paraḥ saṁyatendriyaḥ
jñānaṁ labdhvā parāṁ śāntim acireṇādhigacchati
shlokas.translation: A faithful person who is absorbed in transcendental knowledge and who subdues the senses quickly attains the supreme peace.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna states that a person full of faith who is dedicated to knowledge and controls the senses attains supreme peace without delay. This verse gives the formula for peace. For people in their thirties seeking calm amid chaos this verse is the direct path. For example a parent who studies wisdom faithfully and manages impulses finds quick inner tranquility. Detailed meaning: Krishna links faith dedication and sense control to the immediate gift of supreme peace. This beautiful assurance motivates disciplined practice. The verse makes readers eager to embody these qualities. Practice: cultivate faith in knowledge and steady your senses so supreme peace arrives swiftly.
shlokas.genz: Krishna states that a person full of trust who dives into knowledge and tames the senses reaches supreme peace fast. This verse is the quick map to calm. For young adults craving real rest this verse feels doable. For example someone who trusts the path studies deeply and calms scrolling finds peace showing up soon. Detailed meaning: Krishna connects trust full focus and sense mastery to the fast gift of highest peace. This beautiful promise encourages steady steps. The verse makes you eager to build these habits. Practice: grow trust in knowledge and quiet your senses so the deepest calm comes without waiting.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna states that a person full of belief who loves knowledge and calms the senses reaches the biggest peace very soon. This verse is the happy map to calm. For children this is like trusting good stories learning them and sitting still to feel the big quiet joy. For example a child who believes and listens quietly feels peace right away. Detailed meaning: Krishna links belief deep love and calm senses to the quick gift of highest peace. This beautiful promise makes learning fun. The verse makes you curious to try. Practice: believe in good stories love them and calm your body so the biggest happy peace comes fast.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति |
नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
ajñaś cāśraddadhānaś ca saṁśayātmā vinaśyati
nāyaṁ loko ’sti na paro na sukhaṁ saṁśayātmanaḥ
shlokas.translation: But ignorant and faithless persons who doubt the revealed scriptures go to ruin. For the doubting soul there is happiness neither in this world nor in the next.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna warns that the ignorant faithless and doubting person perishes and finds no happiness in this world or the next. This verse contrasts the fate of the doubter. For people in their thirties facing uncertainty this verse encourages firm faith. For example someone who embraces knowledge without constant doubt builds a stable successful life. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows that doubt destroys both material and spiritual progress. This beautiful warning protects the seeker. The verse makes readers eager to choose faith over skepticism. Practice: replace doubt with trust in transcendental knowledge so happiness flows in every realm.
shlokas.genz: Krishna warns that the unknowing faithless and doubting person falls apart and finds no real joy here or beyond. This verse contrasts the heavy cost of doubt. For young adults questioning everything this verse offers balance. For example someone who trusts the deeper teachings instead of endless skepticism builds a lighter happier path. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that doubt blocks all progress and joy. This beautiful caution guides away from ruin. The verse makes you eager to stand in trust. Practice: trade endless questions for steady belief in knowledge so joy arrives in every space.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna warns that the unknowing faithless and doubting person feels sad and finds no happy home here or after. This verse contrasts the heavy feeling of doubt. For children this is like not believing in good stories and feeling alone. For example a child who trusts the wise tales feels safe and joyful everywhere. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows that doubt stops all fun and peace. This beautiful caution keeps the heart open. The verse makes you curious to believe. Practice: choose trust in happy stories instead of doubt so joy lives in every game.

shlokas.sanskrit:
योगसन्न्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् |
आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yoga-sannyasta-karmāṇaṁ jñāna-sañchinna-saṁśayam
ātmavantaṁ na karmāṇi nibadhnanti dhanañjaya
shlokas.translation: One who acts in devotional service renouncing the fruits of his actions and whose doubts have been destroyed by transcendental knowledge is situated factually in the self. Thus he is not bound by the reactions of work O conqueror of riches.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that the person who has renounced the fruits of action through yoga whose doubts have been cut by knowledge and who is firmly established in the self is not bound by any reactions of work. This verse concludes the teaching on how transcendental knowledge frees one completely. For people in their thirties who handle complex responsibilities this verse offers the ultimate freedom. For example a senior professional who performs every task as an offering without attachment to promotion or recognition experiences no inner turmoil even during high pressure projects. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows that when knowledge destroys doubt and yoga renounces results the soul remains untouched by karma. This beautiful culmination of transcendental knowledge proves that true action is liberating. The verse makes readers eager to live this freedom. Practice: renounce the fruits of your work through yoga and let knowledge cut all doubts so that no action binds you anymore.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that the person who has dropped attachment to results through yoga whose doubts have been slashed by knowledge and who rests firmly in the self is not tied by any karma reactions. This verse wraps the teaching on how transcendental knowledge sets you free. For young adults navigating hustle and choices this verse feels like the final unlock. For example a creator who offers every project without chasing validation or metrics stays calm and creative no matter the feedback. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that knowledge clears doubt and yoga releases outcomes so the self stays untouched. This beautiful end of transcendental knowledge proves action can be pure freedom. The verse makes you eager to live it. Practice: drop attachment to outcomes through yoga and let knowledge clear every doubt so nothing ties you down.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that the person who has given up wanting prizes through yoga whose doubts have been cut by knowledge and who stays happy in the self is not tied by any play troubles. This verse finishes the teaching on how transcendental knowledge makes you free. For children this is like playing without caring who wins and feeling light inside. For example a child who shares toys without expecting anything back stays happy no matter what. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that knowledge removes doubt and yoga lets go of prizes so the self stays free. This beautiful ending of transcendental knowledge shows play can be light. The verse makes you curious to try. Practice: give up wanting prizes through yoga and let knowledge clear doubts so no play troubles touch you.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः |
छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ||
shlokas.transliteration:
tasmād ajñāna-sambhūtaṁ hṛt-sthaṁ jñānāsinātmanaḥ
chittvainaṁ saṁśayaṁ yogam ātiṣṭhottiṣṭha bhārata
shlokas.translation: Therefore the doubts which have arisen in your heart out of ignorance should be slashed by the weapon of knowledge. Armed with yoga O Bhārata stand and fight.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna urges Arjuna to cut the doubt born of ignorance in his heart with the sword of knowledge take shelter in yoga and arise to perform his duty. This verse is the final call to action. For people in their thirties facing inner conflicts this verse gives the decisive step. For example a leader tormented by career doubts who studies wisdom applies it and moves forward with confidence achieves breakthroughs. Detailed meaning: Krishna instructs using transcendental knowledge as a weapon to destroy doubt and then standing firm in yoga. This beautiful conclusion of the chapter motivates immediate application. The verse makes readers eager to rise and act. Practice: identify any doubt in your heart cut it with knowledge take refuge in yoga and stand up to fulfill your responsibilities.
shlokas.genz: Krishna urges Arjuna to slash the doubt born of ignorance in his heart with the sword of knowledge take shelter in yoga and stand up to move forward. This verse is the final push. For young adults stuck in overthinking this verse feels like the wake up. For example someone confused about life direction who learns the truth applies it and takes action breaks through. Detailed meaning: Krishna tells to use transcendental knowledge as a blade to destroy doubt and then stand in yoga. This beautiful ending of the chapter calls for action now. The verse makes you eager to rise. Practice: spot doubt in your mind cut it with knowledge take shelter in yoga and stand up to do what is right.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna urges Arjuna to cut the doubt born of not knowing in his heart with the sword of knowledge take shelter in yoga and stand up to play bravely. This verse is the final happy push. For children this is like a big friend saying cut the scared feeling with smart stories take yoga help and stand to play. For example a child who feels worried about a game learns the right way feels strong and joins happily. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches to use transcendental knowledge as a sharp tool to remove doubt and then stand in yoga. This beautiful close of the chapter invites you to stand tall. The verse makes you curious to rise. Practice: find any worry in your heart cut it with knowledge take yoga help and stand up to play with joy.
